summary_ceo: Co-Founder and Interim CEO of Zephram $20M Oxford-based R&D company building multi-agent and inference technology for scientific research. 12+ years in software engineering, algorithm design and analysis, and former research scientist at Quantinuum and QuEra using machine learning for physics-inspired generative modeling and optimization.
summary_RS: Electrical and Computer Engineering Ph.D. working on machine learning, physics-inspired generative models, and optimization. 12+ years of experience in software engineering and 9+ years in algorithm design and analysis.
summary_RS_cas: I mainly work on machine learning, physics-inspired generative models, and optimization. I occasionally work on computational physics and math too. 12+ years of experience in software engineering and 9+ years in algorithm design and analysis.
summary_op: Electrical and Computer Engineering Ph.D. with 12+ years of experience in software engineering and 9+ years in algorithm design and analysis. Leader of a team of 16 data scientists and engineers developing breakthroughs in machine learning assisted optimization algorithms for applications in computational electromagnetics and quantum optics.
summary_web: Machine Learning Ph.D. candidate with 12+ years of experience in software engineering and 8+ years in algorithm design and analysis. Leader of a team of 16 data scientists and engineers, developing breakthroughs in machine learning with applications in generative AI, computer vision, and quantum.
summary_qml: Quantum machine learning Ph.D. candidate and founder of the NanoML team developing machine learning algorithms for quantum generative AI and photonics. 12+ years of experience in software engineering and 8+ years in algorithm design and analysis.
statementTeaching:
My teaching connects rigorous fundamentals in electrical engineering, computing, and machine learning with research-grounded examples from photonics, optimization, and quantum-adjacent systems. I emphasize clear explanations, hands-on computation, and inclusive mentorship.

I have served in graduate and undergraduate instructional roles at Purdue (ECE courses and research mentorship) and have mentored 200+ researchers through SURF, SoCET, and independent projects, with many students earning first-time researcher and poster awards.
statementResearch:
My research spans machine learning, physics-informed modeling, and optimization for engineered systems—from nanophotonics and semiconductor verification to quantum circuit synthesis and multi-agent scientific inference.

Methodologically I focus on scalable algorithms (including CUDA-accelerated workflows), generative and simulation-based modeling, and collaboration across disciplines. Affiliations and experience include Purdue NanoML, the Quantum Science Center, QuEra, Quantinuum, and industry R&D leadership.
interests: Machine Learning and Generative Modeling for Multiphysics and Design

content.text: Appointed by the board (Abstract) to lead our $20M company during a $1.2M financial investigation and restructuring following insurmountable fiduciary breaches from previous leadership. Managed legal counsel and stakeholder communications to stabilize the entity. Initiated rebranding, stabilized operations, and pivoted to a new business model and product strategy. Brought in research partnerships with Cambridge, UC Berkeley, and Purdue.

As CTO, led a team of 8 Oxford-based engineers and scientists to build and deploy full-stack AI infrastructure for multi-agent systems and inference, spanning CUDA kernels, model serving, backend services, and web interfaces.

Proprietary Breakthroughs: Context compaction (5x speed-up + 80% cost reduction), simulated finetuning ($0.01 vs $1000x LORA), multi-agent swarm communication protocols (15% faster on small-medium scale models), CUDA kernels for multi-GPU optimization (300x speed-up over previous state-of-the-art), and more.

Built and benchmarked multiGPU CUDA async kernels for UCCSD quantum circuits, achieving 4-20x speed-up for data collection. Modified token vocabulary and PPO/GRPO losses for better generative performance in GPT2 models.

Led RL infrastructure and algorithms for collaborating with Nvidia + Deepmind on generative modeling, quantum circuit generation, quantum chemistry and optimization that will fuel future publications and industry partnerships.

Developed generative AI for integrated photonics devices, new latent graph diffusion models for quantum circuit synthesis and transpilation, graph optimization for neural architecture search, and QNLP diagram generation.

Published machine learning algorithms for designing meta-optical devices, achieving a 16200% speed-up resulting in a major milestone for the Quantum Science Center with two news articles and 30 citations.
Raised $27k in computing resources through grants, saved $40k in ML costs through server maintenance, and assisted in raising $200k+ for the QSC Summer Schools.
Led the development of attention-based models for tackling the $75B counterfeit chip market leading to a 55,000x speed-up and 41% accuracy increase in the verification of semiconductor devices.
Demonstrated comparative advantage on neutral atoms for generative AI sampling of photonic devices.
Set team research objectives and wrote quantum simulation software in Python and Julia to simulate Rydberg atom systems for analysis.
Verified early-access, open-source, flagship neutral atom simulation software Bloqade on machine learning sampling tasks.
Derived optimal UAV routes for search and rescue missions to choose deliveries based on a tradeoff of risk of failure and reward.
Created sweep-covers for partitioning road-networks for multi-UAV routing to capture ground-vehicles.

Engineered verification software in Python, XML, and C++ for ARMv8 instruction set coverage, leading to a 250% improvement in Chi-Square randomness and immediate positive feedback from customers for improving test consistency.
Rebuilt 2 ARMv8 debug instruction tests in ARM's verification suite to reduce the complexity of debugging for customers.
Designed a 32-Bit MIPS RISC processor in Verilog with L1-Cache and 5-stage pipeline. Compiled C to MIPS IS and benchmarked on FPGAs.
Developed Python to RISCV transpiler for generating Verilog Read-Only-Memory for RISCV-based System-on-Chip.
